raw_consumer_statement: I filed a fraud report on a charge that I did not make. While Citi did attempt to contact me three times regarding signing the attestation that the charge is indeed fraud, I wasnt able to call back. They also sent two emails with the information, but the links expired and I was unable to view/sign what was necessary. When I got a letter saying the charge was deemed to be valid, I called and told the representative that I wanted to appeal the incorrect decision because I did not make the charge. I specifically mentioned that I legally deserved a credit, at the very least temporarily, until the case is actually resolved. I told the representative I didnt want the fraud charge being reported to the credit bureaus as a balance because the charge was incredibly high dollar, and she confirmed it wouldnt be. I got my statement and the balance does reflect the fraudulent charge and my minimum payment was calculated off of the full statement balance. This is an FDCPA violation and if it is not corrected, I will take further steps, including exercising my ability to a private right of action. I did not make this charge and the information provided by the merchant is lacking facts as it suits them in their chargeback attempt. Ask the merchant what the shipping address was, because nothing was shipped to my residence. The information provided by the merchant intentionally leaves out the shipping address and only includes billing address ( which clearly the fraudster had ). I am protected by FDCPA and Citis zero fraud liability policy in the terms and conditions of the card. I expect Citi to recognize its legal obligations in regard to consumer protection regulations.
